title: Crate Compression Production Contract

included: LZ4 buffer envelope decoding with 0-chunk and N-chunk forms
Compressed integer array decoding for 32-bit and 64-bit element widths
Two-level structure (outer LZ4 envelope around inner codepoint encoding)
Codepoint table dispatch (00, 01, 10, 11) with width-pair semantics
Signed common value regardless of array signedness
Compressed float array primitives reusable by usdc-value-decoder
deferred: compressed float lookup-table 't' form value canonicalization (the integer-indices substream is owned here; value emission is owned by usdc-value-decoder)

lz4_buffer.errata_note: AOUSD §16.3.7.1 prose suggests a u64 chunk count + per-chunk u64 size. Real .usdc files (from the reference USD writer's pxr_lz4::CompressToBuffer / TfFastCompression) use a u8 chunk count + per-chunk i32 size. The TfFastCompression framing below is normative for this repository; the spec text is the errata. A reader must not also accept the spec-text u64+u64 framing as a fallback for the same reason given in the value-representation flag-bit errata: tolerating both layouts hides the divergence and lets one decoder pass two contradictory suites.

zero_chunk_form.payload: the remainder of the buffer is one LZ4 stream decompressed as a whole

multi_chunk_form.payload: chunk_count chunks; each chunk is prefixed by a 4B signed 32-bit little-endian compressed size followed by that many LZ4-compressed bytes

laws: decode_lz4_buffer must accept both chunk_count == 0 and chunk_count > 0 forms; rejecting either is a contract violation
implementations must use a real LZ4 decoder (lz4_decompress_safe or equivalent); a byte-at-a-time interpreter is a contract violation per the performance contract

the LZ4 envelope uses TfFastCompression framing (u8 chunk count, per-chunk i32 size) as normative for this repository; see docs/usdc-spec-errata.md#2. Accepting the spec-text u64+u64 framing as a fallback is a contract violation.

outer_envelope.lz4_payload: compressed_size bytes of LZ4-compressed data following the size field; the decompressed bytes form the inner codepoint encoding

common_value.signedness: stored as the signed version of the array's integer width regardless of whether the array element type is signed or unsigned
common_value.purpose: the most common value in the array of differences; used when codepoint 00 is decoded

codepoints.stream_layout: packed 2-bit codes, one per array element, padded to a byte boundary at the end

01.name: Quarter width signed integer
01.decoded_difference_type: signed int8 for 32-bit arrays; signed int16 for 64-bit arrays

10.name: Half width signed integer
10.decoded_difference_type: signed int16 for 32-bit arrays; signed int32 for 64-bit arrays

11.name: Full width signed integer
11.decoded_difference_type: signed int32 for 32-bit arrays; signed int64 for 64-bit arrays

inner_encoding.values_stream: concatenated per-codepoint values in the order their codepoints appear; codepoint 00 contributes no bytes
inner_encoding.delta_reconstruction: running sum of decoded differences; the first difference is added to 0 (§16.3.7.2.2: 'If no previous value exists, it starts at 0')

laws: the outer envelope is LZ4 (with an 8B compressed-size prefix); skipping the LZ4 decompression step is a contract violation
common value is signed at the array's element width even when the array element type is unsigned; reading common value as unsigned produces wrong deltas for negative jumps
codepoint width pairs differ between 32-bit and 64-bit arrays (01/10/11 ⇒ i8/i16/i32 for 32b vs i16/i32/i64 for 64b); using the same width pair for both is a contract violation
codepoint stream padding must be zero-padded to a byte boundary; non-zero padding is an error
decoding is linear in the element count using table dispatch; per-element branch chains on the 2-bit code are a contract violation per the performance contract

values.i: integer-encoded — uses the compressed integer array path on the array's integer-equivalent width
values.t: lookup-table-encoded — followed by 4B LUT count, count * sizeof(element) value bytes, then a compressed integer array of indices into the LUT

i_form.description: the float array is representable losslessly as an integer array; the binary-format unit provides the integer values, and usdc-value-decoder canonicalizes them as the requested float type
i_form.primitive_reuse: this contract's compressed integer array primitives are reused; usdc-value-decoder must not reimplement the codepoint decoder

t_form.lut_count: unsigned 32-bit integer giving the number of distinct values in the LUT
t_form.lut_values: lut_count contiguous values of the float type's underlying size (4B for float, 8B for double, 2B for half)
t_form.lut_indices: a compressed integer array of length equal to the array's element count, each entry an Index into the LUT
t_form.primitive_reuse: the index substream uses this contract's compressed integer array primitive on uint32

laws: i-form compressed float arrays must reuse this contract's compressed integer array decoder; reimplementing the codepoint decoder in usdc-value-decoder is a contract violation
t-form lookup table indices are compressed integer arrays of uint32 (Index); they must use this contract's decoder
this contract owns LZ4 envelopes and compressed integer array decoding; section traversal and value canonicalization are deferred
all numeric reads are single little-endian word reads; byte-at-a-time scalar loops in the hot path are a contract violation per the performance contract
decompression output is owned (mmap-backed input cannot be aliased after LZ4 decompression); the owned decompressed buffer is exposed to consumers as a non-owning view for the lifetime of the read
golden_groups: compressed integer array exercising codepoints 01, 10, 11 in the same payload (int32 width)
compressed integer array exercising codepoints 01, 10, 11 in the same payload (int64 width)
compressed integer array with codepoint 00 (common value) for at least one element
compressed integer array on an unsigned target with a negative common value (signedness boundary)
compressed float array, t-form (LUT) with at least three repeated values to exercise dedup
